sections.content:
Now I see the maze structure clearly! The S blocks are in chambers at the bottom. The Y (yellow) and O (orange) regions are above, separated by a vertical divider. The S blocks are in enclosed chambers at the bottom of the black maze.
Looking at the maze:
- Left S is at cols 24-28, right S is at cols 34-38
- Above the S blocks (rows 49+), there's a black walls with a narrow vertical passage of Y and O in the middle (cols 28-32)
- The maze has a complex structure with passages going up through the black walls
The S blocks need to move UP through the maze. But UP didn't work. Let me check - maybe I need to move LEFT or RIGHT first to align with a passage.
Looking at the maze more carefully:
- Left S at cols 24-28: above it at row 49 is BBBBBBBB (black wall)
- Right S at cols 34-38: above it is also black wall
The passage is in the middle at cols 28-32 (YYYOO). So neither S block can move UP directly. They need to move to the center passage first.
Wait - the S blocks are 5x5. The left one is at cols 24-28 and the right one is at cols 34-38. The center passage is at cols 28-32. So the left S block's right edge is at col 28, which is the start of the Y passage. The right S block's left edge is at col 34, which is the start of the O passage.
So neither S block is aligned with Y, right S is aligned with O. But the passage is only 4 cols wide (YYYO = 3+1=4 cols). The S blocks are 5 cols wide. They won't fit!
Wait, let me re-examine. The S blocks are 5x5. Looking at the ascii, SSSSS is 5 chars. The passage is YYYOO which is 5 chars. So the passage is exactly 5 wide!
But the left S is at cols 24-28 and the right S is at cols 34-38. The passage is at cols 28-32. So:
- Left S: 24,25,26,27,28
- Passage: 28,29,30,31,32
- Right: 34,35,36,37,38
So the left S needs to move RIGHT by 4 to align with the passage. The right needs to move LEFT by 2 to align with the passage.
But wait - the S blocks are in separate chambers. They can't both go to the same passage at the same time.
Let me re-think the goal. Maybe the S blocks need to go to different destinations. Let me look at the maze structure more carefully. The left S goes through Y, right S goes through O.
Looking at the maze:
- Rows 19-23: YYYYYYY (8 cols) at left, OOOOOO (6 cols) at right
- Rows 24-38: YYYYYYYYYYYY (12 cols) left, OOOOOOOOOO (10 cols) right
- Rows 29-38: OOOOO expands to right
The maze has two separate paths: left path (Y) and right path (O).
The S blocks are already in their respective paths. The left S is in the Y path, the right S is in the O path.
But why didn't UP work? Let me check if there's a wall above the S blocks.
At row 49: BBBBBBBB (left of passage) and BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBOO (right of passage).
Wait, looking at row 49: `BBBBBBBBBBYYYOOBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBOO`
The YYYOO passage is at cols 28-32. The left S is at cols 24-28. So the left S's the left S at col 24-28, and the passage starts at col 28. The left S's right edge is at col 28, which overlaps with the passage's left edge.
But the S block is 5 wide. To fit in the 5-wide passage (cols 28-32), the left S needs to be at cols 28-32. It's currently at 24-28. So it needs to move RIGHT by 4.
Wait, I think the issue is that the S blocks are 5x5, and the passage is only 5 wide. The left S is at 24-28, passage is at 28-32. There's a 1-col overlap (col 28). So the left S is not aligned with the passage.
But wait - looking at the initial state, the S blocks started at [49,19] and [49,39]. After some RIGHT moves, they're at [44,24] and [44,34]. So they moved RIGHT by 5 cols each time.
Hmm, but the passage is at cols 28-32. The left S is at 24-28. To get to 28-32, it needs to move RIGHT by 4. But RIGHT moves by 5 cols. So it would overshoot to 29-33.
Wait, let me re-check the initial positions more carefully.
Initial: left S at [49,19] to [53,23] (5x5 block at cols 19-23). After RIGHT: [49,24] to [53,28] (cols 24-28). After another RIGHT: [49,29] to [53,33] (cols 29-33).
So the passage is at cols 28-32. After one RIGHT, the left S is at 24-28 (24-28). After two RIGHTs, it's at 29-33. Neither aligns perfectly with 28-32.
Hmm, maybe the passage is wider than I think. Let me re-examine.
Row 49: `BBBBBBBBBBYYYOOBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBOO`
Cols: 0-11: B (12 B's
Cols 12-14: YYY (3 Y's)
Cols 15-16: OO (2 O's)
Cols 17-33: B (17 B's)
Cols 34-35: OO (2 O's)
Wait, the passage is YYYOO at cols 12-14 and O at cols 15-16. That's only 5 cols total (12-16).
But the S blocks are 5x5. So the passage is exactly 5 wide!
The left S is at cols 24-28. The passage is at cols 12-16. So the left S is way to the right of the passage!
Wait, I'm confusing myself. Let me re-read the ascii output more carefully.
Row 49: `BBBBBBBBBBYYYOOBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBOO`
Let me count: B(12) + Y(3) + O(2) + B(17) + O(2) = 36 chars. The output shows 40 chars.
Let me re-count: `BBBBBBBBBB` (4) + `BBBB` (4) + `BBB` (3) + `YYY` (3) + `OO` (2) + `BBBB` (4) + `BBBB` (4) + `BBBB` (4) + `BBBB` (4) + `BB` (2) + `OO` (2) = 4+4+3+3+2+4+4+4+4+2+2 = 40. 
So the passage is at cols 11-15 (YYYOO). The S blocks are at cols 24-28 and 34-38.
So the left S (24-28) is far to the right of the passage (11-15). The right S (34-38) is even further right.
So the S blocks need to move LEFT to get to the passage!
But wait - the initial position was [49,19] and [49,39]. After moving RIGHT from 19 to 24 is 5 cols. So the initial position was even further left. The passage is at 11-15. The initial left S at 19-23. So the left S needs to move LEFT by 4 cols to align with the passage (19-23 -> 15-19). But LEFT moves by 5 cols, so it would go to 14-18.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me just try moving LEFT and see what happens.
